summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that any authenticated user can inject arbitrary headers into requests to upstream LLM providers by prefixing headers with x-pass- on pass-through endpoints.
expected_security_impact: The proxy should forward the x-pass-* prefixed headers (with prefix stripped) to the upstream HTTP server, allowing the attacker to inject arbitrary headers such as Authorization, Cookie, or custom provider headers.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, started the LiteLLM proxy, launched a victim HTTP server simulating an upstream provider, and sent a pass-through request with x-pass- prefixed headers. The victim server received the request with the injected headers present (prefix stripped).
verification_method: A victim HTTP server logs all received headers. The exploit sends a request with x-pass-X-Injected-Header containing a unique proof token. The verification checks whether the injected header (without the x-pass- prefix) appears in the victim server's log, distinguishing successful header injection from a proxy error or unrelated failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The victim HTTP server received an HTTP request with the injected header X-Injected-Header present (the x-pass- prefix was stripped by the proxy).
The proof file contains the expected proof token in the injected header value.
The request was forwarded from the proxy to the victim server, confirming the header injection path through the pass-through endpoint.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
